description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a steep, multi-gabled red-tiled roof and a brick exterior. The structure features five stories, each with rows of rectangular windows that are symmetrically arranged on all sides. The front facade includes a central entrance and a series of balconies on the upper floors, while the rear and side facades display a mix of window groupings and some decorative brickwork. Two prominent chimneys rise from the roof, adding to the building's traditional urban character. The windows are framed in white, contrasting with the dark brown brickwork, and some windows have visible curtains or blinds. The overall design suggests a blend of functional urban housing with classic architectural elements.
description: A five-story brick apartment building with a steep red-tiled roof, symmetrical windows, and balconies on the front facade. Features include two chimneys, white window frames, and a central entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, symmetrical window rows, and three balconies above the entrance.
description_view_1: Side view with a mix of window groupings, no balconies, and two chimneys visible on the roof.
description_view_2: Rear view featuring multiple balconies, symmetrical window arrangement, and both chimneys visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with grouped windows, no balconies, and both chimneys visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in symmetrical vertical and horizontal rows, with consistent spacing and alignment across all facades. Some facades feature grouped windows and balconies, while others have evenly distributed single windows.
